replication_study.hypothesis: The median of group-average market prices in the UNIFORM treatment differs from 1.94 (two-sided Wilcoxon signed-rank), with an a priori expectation that it is greater than 1.94.

data_plan.dataset_identifier: oTree IPO Auction Replication (Round 1 and Round 2) and Zhang group averages
data_plan.source_type: Laboratory experiment (oTree exports); original figure-based group averages CSV
data_plan.wave_or_subset: Two independent experimental sessions (Round 1 and Round 2), 20 rounds each; analysis excludes practice/incomplete groups (group.id_in_subsession != 1) and filters out groups flagged as dropout or bankrupt.
data_plan.sample_size: Approx. 40 groups in the combined collapsed dataset (fullsample_analysis.csv had 40 rows); UNIFORM-only groups are used for the focal test.
data_plan.unit_of_analysis: Group-average market price (per session.group), computed over 20 rounds; round-level market prices are available if needed.
data_plan.access_details: Data are provided as CSV exports in replication_data (round1_raw.csv, round2_raw.csv, zhang_avgprice.csv). Analysis assumes these files are available in /app/data at runtime.

qualification.explanation: This dataset contains new experimental sessions implementing the same UNIFORM auction environment as in the original study, enabling an independent replication test of whether the UNIFORM mechanism yields market prices different from the FIXED price benchmark of 1.94 using the same nonparametric test. The inclusion of raw, round-level records allows high-fidelity reconstruction of group-average prices and appropriate filtering for data quality (bankrupt/dropout), while still representing a distinct sample of human subjects and sessions.
qualification.similarity_to_original: Original design details: fixed price in FIXED = 1.94 and UNIFORM auction with market-clearing price, 20 rounds, 3 informed + 1 uninformed per group (post_registration.json: data.source, data.wave_or_subset; original_paper.pdf summary confirms fixed price 1.94 and 20-round sessions). Replication raw data include round-level market prices and market values in variables like 'ipo_task.<round>.group.market_price' and 'ipo_task.<round>.group.market_value', session/group identifiers ('session.code', 'ipo_task.1.group.id_in_subsession') (replication_data/round1_raw.csv and round2_raw.csv columns). The focal test is the Wilcoxon signed-rank against 1.94 (initial_details.txt; post_registration.method.models). 'zhang_avgprice.csv' provides 14 UNIFORM group-average prices consistent with the original reported 14 groups (post_registration.data.sample_size) and is used to reproduce the original finding.
qualification.deviation_from_original: The replication uses new subjects and sessions (Round 1 and Round 2 exports in replication_data) rather than the original session data, providing an independent sample. The platform is an oTree-based implementation (as evidenced by oTree-like column names in round1_raw.csv/round2_raw.csv), whereas the original study environment is not explicitly stated as oTree (original_paper.pdf summary). The combined replication sample appears larger (fullsample_analysis.csv shows 40 groups) than the original 14 UNIFORM groups (post_registration.data.sample_size). While the original analysis compared UNIFORM group averages to the fixed price benchmark (1.94), the replication follows the same test but on newly collected UNIFORM session data.

data_plan.notes: Variables use dot-separated names (e.g., 'ipo_task.7.group.market_price'). Filtering mirrors the original logic: exclude groups with ipo_task.1.group.id_in_subsession == 1; define dropout if rounds_missed_max >= 6 and bankrupt if rounds_missed_min == -99 (derived from 'ipo_task.20.player.total_missing_responses'). All script IOs assume data are in /app/data. zhang_avgprice.csv is used to reproduce the original figure-based test and is not the replication sample. Potential differences in experimental UI/platform and subject pool exist but the key environment and fixed price benchmark are consistent.

steps: Place the following files into /app/data: round1_raw.csv, round2_raw.csv, zhang_avgprice.csv.

Compute the UNIFORM group-average market prices: from each raw CSV, keep completed groups (ipo_task.1.group.id_in_subsession != 1), reshape group.market_price across rounds into long format, compute rounds_missed_max and rounds_missed_min from 'ipo_task.20.player.total_missing_responses', flag dropout (>=6) and bankrupt (== -99), and filter out flagged groups.
Collapse to session.group-level averages of market_price (mean over 20 rounds).
Run a two-sided Wilcoxon signed-rank test on the set of group-average market prices vs mu = 1.94 and report n, statistic, p-value, and median difference.
Optionally, reproduce the original check using zhang_avgprice.csv against 1.94 with the same Wilcoxon signed-rank test.
planned_method.models: Nonparametric one-sample Wilcoxon signed-rank test against mu = 1.94.
planned_method.outcome_variable: Group-average market_price over 20 rounds in the UNIFORM treatment.
planned_method.independent_variables: Not applicable for the focal one-sample test; baseline constant is the fixed price 1.94.
planned_method.tools_software: Python 3.10; pandas for data wrangling; SciPy (scipy.stats.wilcoxon) for Wilcoxon signed-rank test; numpy as dependency.

planned_estimation_and_test.estimation: Median difference of group-average market_price relative to 1.94.
planned_estimation_and_test.test: Two-sided Wilcoxon signed-rank test (scipy.stats.wilcoxon on differences vs 0).

planned_method.missing_data_handling: Listwise deletion of missing market_price observations after reshaping; exclude groups with missing or flagged status (dropout/bankrupt).
planned_method.multiple_testing_policy: Not applicable for the focal analysis; single primary test. If extended outcomes are analyzed, control FDR using Benjamini-Hochberg.
planned_method.inference_criteria: Primary threshold alpha = 0.05 (two-sided). Success criteria: p < 0.05 and positive median difference (expected direction: UNIFORM > 1.94).

files.replication_data/analysis_plan__py.py: Python translation of analysis_plan.do to reproduce the original figure-based test. Reads /app/data/zhang_avgprice.csv (column 'avgprice'), runs a two-sided Wilcoxon signed-rank test against mu=1.94, and prints n, statistic, p-value, and median difference.
files.replication_data/replication_analysis__p: Python translation/wrapper of replication_analysis.do and replication_analysis.Rmd. Reads /app/data/round1_raw.csv and /app/data/round2_raw.csv; reshapes 'ipo_task.<round>.group.market_price' to long; computes rounds_missed_max/min from 'ipo_task.20.player.total_missing_responses'; flags and filters dropout/bankrupt; collapses to group-average market_price; runs a two-sided Wilcoxon signed-rank test vs 1.94; saves /app/data/round1_analysis.csv and /app/data/fullsample_analysis.csv.

codebase.notes: All IO paths are constrained to /app/data per run policy. Non-Python scripts (.do, .Rmd) were not modified; instead, Python rewrites are provided alongside them with the suffix '__py.py'. Dependencies: pandas and scipy. Entry point for the replication analysis is replication_data/replication_analysis__py.py.

instructions: Ensure the following files are present in ./data (mapped to /app/data in container): round1_raw.csv, round2_raw.csv, zhang_avgprice.csv.
Optionally check original figure-based result: python data/original/27/input/replication_data/analysis_plan__py.py (or run from project root with PYTHONPATH set appropriately).
Inspect console output for Wilcoxon n, statistic, p-value, and median difference; find saved outputs in /app/data/round1_analysis.csv and /app/data/fullsample_analysis.csv.
comparison_metrics: Effect direction (sign of median difference: UNIFORM median group-average price minus 1.94).
Robustness to exclusion criteria (results stable after excluding dropout/bankrupt).
If desired, share of groups with average price below 1.94 (compare to original: 2 of 14 groups; see original_paper.pdf summary and post_registration.json).
